title: Shipped: Review Queue draft-triage UI in the VS Code extension
context: Implements the build committed in dec_df253f50b4. Kept the extension a pure reader: the partition logic (parseSynth/reviewQueue) is ported into hunchData.ts as pure offline shaping, and every accept/reject/batch delegates to the `hunch review` CLI via a single runHunch choke-point — the extension never mutates .hunch/ JSON itself (honors the Delegate-all-writes invariant). Webview is CSP-locked and self-contained, mirroring graph.ts. Verified: reviewQueue over the live .hunch/ matches the CLI exactly (0 ready, 10 scrutiny, identical ids/order).
decision: Added a Review Queue webview to the VS Code extension (hunch.review command + inbox button in the Hunch view title bar). It renders the same READY / SCRUTINY split as `hunch review` as a card list — each draft shows title, decision text, rejected alternatives, badges (status/topic/critic-verified/confidence/grounded) and clickable related files — with per-card Accept / Reject and batch Accept-all-verified / Reject-near-duplicates. Delivers the roadmap intent in dec_df253f50b4 (draft triage is the one moment a human must be in the loop).
consequences: Draft triage no longer requires copying decision ids into a shell.
Inline edit remains unbuilt until a CLI edit verb lands — tracked as follow-up.
The strategy veto (no standalone web app, dec_df253f50b4) stays live on its own topic.
alternatives_rejected: Let the extension write .hunch/ JSON directly for speed — violates the Delegate-all-writes invariant; all writes must go through the CLI's atomic paths.
Inline draft EDITING in v1 — deferred: no `hunch review --edit` CLI verb exists, and the no-direct-write invariant forbids the extension patching JSON. Edit needs a new CLI command first.
